channel.title: The Morning Lift
channel.description: Four positive stories in four minutes. Free audio, waiting for you when you wake up. Every morning, The Morning Lift brings you four uplifting, true stories — science, progress, kindness, and quiet wonder — to start your day on the right note.
channel.itunes:summary: Four positive stories in four minutes. Free audio, waiting for you when you wake up. Every morning, The Morning Lift brings you four uplifting, true stories — science, progress, kindness, and quiet wonder — to start your day on the right note.
channel.itunes:author: The Morning Lift

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, July 22, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) El Salvador eliminates trachoma, the world's leading infectious cause of blindness; (2) Homegrown catnip lotion matches DEET as a mosquito repellent in rural Uganda; (3) Spain's 'firefighting donkeys' have kept Doñana National Park fire-free for nine years; (4) Scans show Egyptian princesses buried 4,000 years ago were skilled archers.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) El Salvador eliminates trachoma, the world's leading infectious cause of blindness; (2) Homegrown catnip lotion matches DEET as a mosquito repellent in rural Uganda; (3) Spain's 'firefighting donkeys' have kept Doñana National Park fire-free for nine years; (4) Scans show Egyptian princesses buried 4,000 years ago were skilled archers.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, July 21, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Scientists name a tenth "walking" shark off Papua New Guinea; (2) 800 million people have gained electricity access since 2010; (3) A lonely 4-year-old's daily wave knit his whole street into a family; (4) Glow-worms return to South Downs National Park.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Scientists name a tenth "walking" shark off Papua New Guinea; (2) 800 million people have gained electricity access since 2010; (3) A lonely 4-year-old's daily wave knit his whole street into a family; (4) Glow-worms return to South Downs National Park.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, July 20, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A therapist and a stroke patient walk as one through linked robotic legs; (2) A new blood test detects womb cancer with 99% accuracy — sparing invasive exams — Health/MedTech; (3) River otters return to once-toxic city waters as a living clean-water scorecard; (4) Reno neighbors turn a dirt lot into a park with a $5,000 grant.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A therapist and a stroke patient walk as one through linked robotic legs; (2) A new blood test detects womb cancer with 99% accuracy — sparing invasive exams — Health/MedTech; (3) River otters return to once-toxic city waters as a living clean-water scorecard; (4) Reno neighbors turn a dirt lot into a park with a $5,000 grant.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, July 19, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) The malaria-vaccine inventor wins Europe's top invention prize; (2) Sea turtles are having a record-smashing nesting summer; (3) Egypt uncovers a "lost" Byzantine city in the Dakhla Oasis; (4) New material makes heat "programmable".
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) The malaria-vaccine inventor wins Europe's top invention prize; (2) Sea turtles are having a record-smashing nesting summer; (3) Egypt uncovers a "lost" Byzantine city in the Dakhla Oasis; (4) New material makes heat "programmable".

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, July 18, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Drilling with microwaves to tap "unlimited" geothermal heat; (2) Largest-ever gravitational-wave catalog: 161 new black-hole collisions; (3) Bezos Earth Fund puts $100M into turning vacant lots into parks in 8 cities; (4) Cameron Myers, 20, becomes the fastest Australian ever over 1500m.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Drilling with microwaves to tap "unlimited" geothermal heat; (2) Largest-ever gravitational-wave catalog: 161 new black-hole collisions; (3) Bezos Earth Fund puts $100M into turning vacant lots into parks in 8 cities; (4) Cameron Myers, 20, becomes the fastest Australian ever over 1500m.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, July 17, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Why some brains resist Alzheimer's: "immature" neurons that switch on survival genes; (2) Grand Rapids puts the rapids back in its river; (3) Hamilton's First Bank of the United States reopens as a free museum; (4) Special Olympics Unified Football World Cup lights up Paris.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Why some brains resist Alzheimer's: "immature" neurons that switch on survival genes; (2) Grand Rapids puts the rapids back in its river; (3) Hamilton's First Bank of the United States reopens as a free museum; (4) Special Olympics Unified Football World Cup lights up Paris.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, July 16, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) James Webb solves the "pink planet" mystery — the answer is clouds of salt — Science/Astronomy; (2) West Marin opens 14 homes for families displaced by ranch closures; (3) Shohei Ohtani hits his 300th home run — 5th-fastest ever, while also a pitcher — Sports/Baseball; (4) A fossil "mother goose of the mud" rewrites New Zealand's bird history.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) James Webb solves the "pink planet" mystery — the answer is clouds of salt — Science/Astronomy; (2) West Marin opens 14 homes for families displaced by ranch closures; (3) Shohei Ohtani hits his 300th home run — 5th-fastest ever, while also a pitcher — Sports/Baseball; (4) A fossil "mother goose of the mud" rewrites New Zealand's bird history.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, July 15, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A man with ALS is speaking again in his own voice through a brain implant — Innovation/Brain-Computer Interface; (2) Astronomers witness the birth of a magnetar for the first time; (3) Largest-ever "Food as Medicine" study shows food banks cut hospital visits; (4) Linda Noskova, 21, wins Wimbledon after her opponent saves five championship points.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A man with ALS is speaking again in his own voice through a brain implant — Innovation/Brain-Computer Interface; (2) Astronomers witness the birth of a magnetar for the first time; (3) Largest-ever "Food as Medicine" study shows food banks cut hospital visits; (4) Linda Noskova, 21, wins Wimbledon after her opponent saves five championship points.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, July 14, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A daily pill nearly doubles survival in metastatic pancreatic cancer; (2) Poland's national grid gets its first-ever offshore wind electricity; (3) Three untouched 13th-century shipwrecks surface in Menorca's "Cove of Mysteries"; (4) Kenya's Wanyonyi breaks a 27-year-old 1000m world record — in his first race at the distance — Sports/Athletics.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A daily pill nearly doubles survival in metastatic pancreatic cancer; (2) Poland's national grid gets its first-ever offshore wind electricity; (3) Three untouched 13th-century shipwrecks surface in Menorca's "Cove of Mysteries"; (4) Kenya's Wanyonyi breaks a 27-year-old 1000m world record — in his first race at the distance — Sports/Athletics.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, July 13, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A frog-gut bacterium cleared colorectal tumors in every mouse it was tested on; (2) An onion farmer's son becomes the fourth Indian ever to medal at a Diamond League; (3) Euclid finds the most ancient quasars ever seen, deepening the early-black-hole puzzle; (4) A jacket that pulls drinking water straight from the air.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A frog-gut bacterium cleared colorectal tumors in every mouse it was tested on; (2) An onion farmer's son becomes the fourth Indian ever to medal at a Diamond League; (3) Euclid finds the most ancient quasars ever seen, deepening the early-black-hole puzzle; (4) A jacket that pulls drinking water straight from the air.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, July 12, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A rebuilt MRI antenna makes existing scanners see sharper; (2) Cincinnati community overshoots its goal to build a 14-home veterans village; (3) Matt Olson plays his 741st straight game to become the Braves' Iron Man; (4) A 1611 Brueghel painting recorded a bat behavior science confirmed only in 2025.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A rebuilt MRI antenna makes existing scanners see sharper; (2) Cincinnati community overshoots its goal to build a 14-home veterans village; (3) Matt Olson plays his 741st straight game to become the Braves' Iron Man; (4) A 1611 Brueghel painting recorded a bat behavior science confirmed only in 2025.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, July 11, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Europe's air is measurably cleaner than a decade ago; (2) 800 million people have gained electricity access since 2010; (3) Britain's Cerne Abbas Giant is being re-chalked; (4) 80+ volunteers restored eight Scranton seniors' homes in a day.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Europe's air is measurably cleaner than a decade ago; (2) 800 million people have gained electricity access since 2010; (3) Britain's Cerne Abbas Giant is being re-chalked; (4) 80+ volunteers restored eight Scranton seniors' homes in a day.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, July 10, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A silicon chip that writes DNA in water; (2) The ocean has been braking global drought for over a century; (3) AI predicted two new superconductors before they were made; (4) A British wildcard into his first Wimbledon semifinal.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A silicon chip that writes DNA in water; (2) The ocean has been braking global drought for over a century; (3) AI predicted two new superconductors before they were made; (4) A British wildcard into his first Wimbledon semifinal.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, July 9, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Kelsey Pfendler — first American woman to row solo from California to Hawaii; (2) Dolly Parton musical sets Broadway opening — on her 81st birthday; (3) Montana Bar Fairies — gift cards instead of tickets for people who didn't drive drunk; (4) New walking shark species confirmed off Papua New Guinea.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Kelsey Pfendler — first American woman to row solo from California to Hawaii; (2) Dolly Parton musical sets Broadway opening — on her 81st birthday; (3) Montana Bar Fairies — gift cards instead of tickets for people who didn't drive drunk; (4) New walking shark species confirmed off Papua New Guinea.

item.title: Mid Week Lift — Wednesday, July 8, 2026
item.description: A midweek bonus from The Morning Lift: “Saturn's rotation puzzle solved by JWST (Science/Planetary)” — one of our most-loved stories, re-read for you. Originally from our May 31 episode. Hear a new lift every morning at themorninglift.org.
item.itunes:summary: A midweek bonus from The Morning Lift: “Saturn's rotation puzzle solved by JWST (Science/Planetary)” — one of our most-loved stories, re-read for you. Originally from our May 31 episode. Hear a new lift every morning at themorninglift.org.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, July 8, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Paris reopens the Seine to public swimming; (2) Lost Leonora Carrington painting "Villa Pilar" goes on public view; (3) Astronomers find DF9, a third dark-matter-free galaxy on a collision trail; (4) Duke-NUS identifies DEAF1, the gene "switch" behind exercise reversing muscle aging.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Paris reopens the Seine to public swimming; (2) Lost Leonora Carrington painting "Villa Pilar" goes on public view; (3) Astronomers find DF9, a third dark-matter-free galaxy on a collision trail; (4) Duke-NUS identifies DEAF1, the gene "switch" behind exercise reversing muscle aging.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, July 7, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Perovskite-silicon solar cell hits a certified 34.82% efficiency; (2) Quentin Blake Centre for Illustration opens in London; (3) Tour de France 2026 opens with a Grand Départ in Barcelona; (4) Kākāpō have their biggest breeding season on record.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Perovskite-silicon solar cell hits a certified 34.82% efficiency; (2) Quentin Blake Centre for Illustration opens in London; (3) Tour de France 2026 opens with a Grand Départ in Barcelona; (4) Kākāpō have their biggest breeding season on record.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, July 6, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A 22-year-old's 3D-printed dentures; (2) The marathon-running nun's food pantry; (3) Ann Patchett wins the Library of Congress Prize; (4) Low-inflammatory diet linked to lower dementia risk.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A 22-year-old's 3D-printed dentures; (2) The marathon-running nun's food pantry; (3) Ann Patchett wins the Library of Congress Prize; (4) Low-inflammatory diet linked to lower dementia risk.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, July 5, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) FDA expands Casgevy gene therapy to children as young as two with sickle cell disease; (2) Anonymous "Dutch School" painting revealed as an early Rembrandt after a decade of restoration; (3) Jordan's Aqaba Marine Reserve recommended for UNESCO World Heritage — a heat-resilient coral refuge; (4) Finnish qualifier Otto Virtanen upsets fourth seed Ben Shelton at Wimbledon.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) FDA expands Casgevy gene therapy to children as young as two with sickle cell disease; (2) Anonymous "Dutch School" painting revealed as an early Rembrandt after a decade of restoration; (3) Jordan's Aqaba Marine Reserve recommended for UNESCO World Heritage — a heat-resilient coral refuge; (4) Finnish qualifier Otto Virtanen upsets fourth seed Ben Shelton at Wimbledon.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, July 4, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Robots fight strawberry-farm pests with UV light instead of pesticides; (2) First synthetic cell to complete a full life cycle — "SpudCell"; (3) Magnetic ripples ("magnons") made to last ~100× longer — toward penny-size quantum computers; (4) Dubuque free community meal returns after six years dark.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Robots fight strawberry-farm pests with UV light instead of pesticides; (2) First synthetic cell to complete a full life cycle — "SpudCell"; (3) Magnetic ripples ("magnons") made to last ~100× longer — toward penny-size quantum computers; (4) Dubuque free community meal returns after six years dark.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, July 3, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Melanoma's "immortality" mechanism cracked — TERT + TPP1; (2) England approves therapy that delays type 1 diabetes up to 3 years; (3) Single injection regrows joint cartilage — 15-PGDH block; (4) DC literacy push lifts 17,000 students.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Melanoma's "immortality" mechanism cracked — TERT + TPP1; (2) England approves therapy that delays type 1 diabetes up to 3 years; (3) Single injection regrows joint cartilage — 15-PGDH block; (4) DC literacy push lifts 17,000 students.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, July 2, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A hidden museum opens beneath the Lincoln Memorial; (2) The Ocean Cleanup scales river Interceptors in the Philippines; (3) UK new electric cars outsell new petrol cars for the first time; (4) A stem-cell-inspired method grows immune cells at scale.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A hidden museum opens beneath the Lincoln Memorial; (2) The Ocean Cleanup scales river Interceptors in the Philippines; (3) UK new electric cars outsell new petrol cars for the first time; (4) A stem-cell-inspired method grows immune cells at scale.

item.title: Mid Week Lift — Wednesday, July 1, 2026
item.description: A midweek bonus from The Morning Lift: “Saturn's rotation puzzle solved by JWST” — one of our most-loved stories, re-read for you. Originally from our May 31 episode. Hear a new lift every morning at themorninglift.org.
item.itunes:summary: A midweek bonus from The Morning Lift: “Saturn's rotation puzzle solved by JWST” — one of our most-loved stories, re-read for you. Originally from our May 31 episode. Hear a new lift every morning at themorninglift.org.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, July 1, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Soil bacteria make crops grow in salty, written-off farmland; (2) Habitat for Humanity breaks ground on its largest-ever neighborhood; (3) Auracast turns public-venue audio into a barrier-free broadcast; (4) DR Congo reaches the World Cup knockout round for the first time.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Soil bacteria make crops grow in salty, written-off farmland; (2) Habitat for Humanity breaks ground on its largest-ever neighborhood; (3) Auracast turns public-venue audio into a barrier-free broadcast; (4) DR Congo reaches the World Cup knockout round for the first time.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, June 30, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Serena Williams returns to Wimbledon singles; (2) Bepirovirsen functionally cures chronic hepatitis B; (3) Brine-free solar desalination; (4) Solid material upconverts visible sunlight into UV.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Serena Williams returns to Wimbledon singles; (2) Bepirovirsen functionally cures chronic hepatitis B; (3) Brine-free solar desalination; (4) Solid material upconverts visible sunlight into UV.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, June 29, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Special Olympics 2026 USA Games close in Minnesota; (2) NASA attempts a first-ever orbital rescue of the Swift telescope; (3) Snow leopards rebound in Kazakhstan to 1980s levels; (4) Largest Roman bath complex ever found in the Netherlands.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Special Olympics 2026 USA Games close in Minnesota; (2) NASA attempts a first-ever orbital rescue of the Swift telescope; (3) Snow leopards rebound in Kazakhstan to 1980s levels; (4) Largest Roman bath complex ever found in the Netherlands.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, June 28, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) HPV vaccine has "almost eliminated" cervical cancer in young women; (2) India halved its smoking rate this century; (3) Reintroduced beavers solved chronic flooding in west London; (4) Chilangas FC: blind women's football transforming lives in Mexico.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) HPV vaccine has "almost eliminated" cervical cancer in young women; (2) India halved its smoking rate this century; (3) Reintroduced beavers solved chronic flooding in west London; (4) Chilangas FC: blind women's football transforming lives in Mexico.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, June 27, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Scotland fan finishes 3,000-mile charity walk across America for mental health; (2) Kew digitizes its entire 7.4-million-specimen herbarium and puts it online for free; (3) Essex firefighters save 5-year-old's fingertips; she returns to thank them; (4) Ancient DNA shows the last Neanderthals weren't doomed by inbreeding.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Scotland fan finishes 3,000-mile charity walk across America for mental health; (2) Kew digitizes its entire 7.4-million-specimen herbarium and puts it online for free; (3) Essex firefighters save 5-year-old's fingertips; she returns to thank them; (4) Ancient DNA shows the last Neanderthals weren't doomed by inbreeding.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, June 26, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A retinal implant restores reading vision in advanced macular degeneration; (2) AARP Community Challenge funds a record 750 quick-action grants; (3) A die stamp found in Kent rewrites the Sutton Hoo helmet's origin; (4) Honeybee "royal cribs": queens are made by their chamber, not just diet.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A retinal implant restores reading vision in advanced macular degeneration; (2) AARP Community Challenge funds a record 750 quick-action grants; (3) A die stamp found in Kent rewrites the Sutton Hoo helmet's origin; (4) Honeybee "royal cribs": queens are made by their chamber, not just diet.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, June 25, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Cape Verde's undefeated World Cup debut; (2) Humpback whale super-groups off Antarctica; (3) AI-designed rare-earth-free magnets; (4) UN marks 20 years of disability-rights law.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Cape Verde's undefeated World Cup debut; (2) Humpback whale super-groups off Antarctica; (3) AI-designed rare-earth-free magnets; (4) UN marks 20 years of disability-rights law.

item.title: Mid Week Lift — Wednesday, June 24, 2026
item.description: A midweek bonus from The Morning Lift: “NASA fires up most powerful electric thruster ever tested in the US — a step toward crewed Mars” — one of our most-loved stories, re-read for you. Originally from our May 14 episode. Hear a new lift every morning at themorninglift.org.
item.itunes:summary: A midweek bonus from The Morning Lift: “NASA fires up most powerful electric thruster ever tested in the US — a step toward crewed Mars” — one of our most-loved stories, re-read for you. Originally from our May 14 episode. Hear a new lift every morning at themorninglift.org.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, June 24, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Carolina Hurricanes win their second Stanley Cup; ~180,000 pack Raleigh for the city's largest-ever parade; (2) Smithsonian's Arts and Industries Building reopens to the public for the first time since 2004; (3) A sodium-ion battery matches key benchmarks of Tesla's lithium cells; (4) Astronomers catch an early-universe quasar flickering — and find a modern-looking disk.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Carolina Hurricanes win their second Stanley Cup; ~180,000 pack Raleigh for the city's largest-ever parade; (2) Smithsonian's Arts and Industries Building reopens to the public for the first time since 2004; (3) A sodium-ion battery matches key benchmarks of Tesla's lithium cells; (4) Astronomers catch an early-universe quasar flickering — and find a modern-looking disk.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, June 23, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) North Carolina and Oklahoma meet in a first-title College World Series finals; (2) Cornell's electrochemical bath revives spent EV batteries to ~95% capacity; (3) Gallup finds Americans see far more everyday kindness than they assume; (4) The Utah Arts Festival survives financial trouble to reach its 50th year.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) North Carolina and Oklahoma meet in a first-title College World Series finals; (2) Cornell's electrochemical bath revives spent EV batteries to ~95% capacity; (3) Gallup finds Americans see far more everyday kindness than they assume; (4) The Utah Arts Festival survives financial trouble to reach its 50th year.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, June 22, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Kyrgyzstan formalizes a "climate-ready" wildlife corridor for snow leopards; (2) The Smithsonian's Arts and Industries Building reopens after 22 years; (3) Researchers 3D-print architectural panels from yeast; (4) Workforce Pell — biggest federal college-aid expansion in 50 years — launches.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Kyrgyzstan formalizes a "climate-ready" wildlife corridor for snow leopards; (2) The Smithsonian's Arts and Industries Building reopens after 22 years; (3) Researchers 3D-print architectural panels from yeast; (4) Workforce Pell — biggest federal college-aid expansion in 50 years — launches.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, June 21, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) North Atlantic right whales record best calving season since 2009; (2) Make Music Day fills streets in 1,000+ cities; (3) Food-rescue groups close the summer school-meal gap; (4) Study: the brain can measurably improve at any age.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) North Atlantic right whales record best calving season since 2009; (2) Make Music Day fills streets in 1,000+ cities; (3) Food-rescue groups close the summer school-meal gap; (4) Study: the brain can measurably improve at any age.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, June 20, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) First in-body CRISPR therapy hits Phase 3 in hereditary angioedema; (2) Mammalian regeneration "switched off, not lost" — two-step FGF2→BMP2 regrows joint tissue; (3) Genicular artery embolization delivers 12 months of knee-arthritis relief — no surgery; (4) "Lucy's hunter": new fossil crocodile shared the riverbanks with our ancestors.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) First in-body CRISPR therapy hits Phase 3 in hereditary angioedema; (2) Mammalian regeneration "switched off, not lost" — two-step FGF2→BMP2 regrows joint tissue; (3) Genicular artery embolization delivers 12 months of knee-arthritis relief — no surgery; (4) "Lucy's hunter": new fossil crocodile shared the riverbanks with our ancestors.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, June 19, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A real weakness found in PFAS "forever chemicals"; (2) A fixable cause of fading cellular energy; (3) A durable middle path for knee osteoarthritis; (4) Housing built over a food pantry and a bus.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A real weakness found in PFAS "forever chemicals"; (2) A fixable cause of fading cellular energy; (3) A durable middle path for knee osteoarthritis; (4) Housing built over a food pantry and a bus.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, June 18, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Messi scores his first-ever World Cup hat trick in his sixth World Cup; (2) A once-a-day pill nearly doubles survival in pancreatic cancer; (3) Two Kenyan teens turn farm waste into a working car-exhaust filter; (4) Cahokia Mounds museum reopens after four-plus years.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Messi scores his first-ever World Cup hat trick in his sixth World Cup; (2) A once-a-day pill nearly doubles survival in pancreatic cancer; (3) Two Kenyan teens turn farm waste into a working car-exhaust filter; (4) Cahokia Mounds museum reopens after four-plus years.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, June 17, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A legendary golden "sea silk," lost for 2,000 years, has been recreated; (2) Battery-free artificial photosynthesis that regulates itself; (3) A single chip that generates, steers, and reads light; (4) Parrots may actually use names to identify individuals.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A legendary golden "sea silk," lost for 2,000 years, has been recreated; (2) Battery-free artificial photosynthesis that regulates itself; (3) A single chip that generates, steers, and reads light; (4) Parrots may actually use names to identify individuals.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, June 16, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Children's reading-for-pleasure rises for the first time since 2021; (2) JUNO neutrino observatory publishes its first physics result; (3) Global mangrove forests rebound — net decline reversing — Environment/Conservation; (4) Angkor Thom's 800-year-old water system uncovered — and slated for restoration — Culture/Archaeology.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Children's reading-for-pleasure rises for the first time since 2021; (2) JUNO neutrino observatory publishes its first physics result; (3) Global mangrove forests rebound — net decline reversing — Environment/Conservation; (4) Angkor Thom's 800-year-old water system uncovered — and slated for restoration — Culture/Archaeology.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, June 15, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Boston extends its fare-free bus program — and the data explains why; (2) Delhi records its cleanest Jan–May air in eight years; (3) "POMbranes" — a filter with perfectly uniform one-nanometer pores; (4) Georgia's women win a second straight NCAA outdoor track title.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Boston extends its fare-free bus program — and the data explains why; (2) Delhi records its cleanest Jan–May air in eight years; (3) "POMbranes" — a filter with perfectly uniform one-nanometer pores; (4) Georgia's women win a second straight NCAA outdoor track title.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, June 14, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) LEAD — West Virginia & Troy reach the College World Series for the first time; (2) Tribeca Festival's record-breaking 25th edition closes; (3) Stanford blocks a "gerozyme" and regrows knee cartilage; (4) 30-year study: ~90–120 min/week of strength training is the high-value zone.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) LEAD — West Virginia & Troy reach the College World Series for the first time; (2) Tribeca Festival's record-breaking 25th edition closes; (3) Stanford blocks a "gerozyme" and regrows knee cartilage; (4) 30-year study: ~90–120 min/week of strength training is the high-value zone.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, June 13, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A power company replanted nearly half a million trees — and 91% survived; (2) For the first full month on record, U.S. solar out-produced coal; (3) A teenager won UK Young Engineer of the Year for a medical-delivery drone; (4) NASA's Roman telescope may launch early — and could find 100,000 new worlds.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A power company replanted nearly half a million trees — and 91% survived; (2) For the first full month on record, U.S. solar out-produced coal; (3) A teenager won UK Young Engineer of the Year for a medical-delivery drone; (4) NASA's Roman telescope may launch early — and could find 100,000 new worlds.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, June 12, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) LEAD; (2) Innovation/Aerospace; (3) Community/Housing (underused-category anchor); (4) Health/Endocrinology.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) LEAD; (2) Innovation/Aerospace; (3) Community/Housing (underused-category anchor); (4) Health/Endocrinology.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, June 11, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Sudan & South Sudan eliminate maternal & neonatal tetanus; (2) Wheelchair half-marathon records in Chicago (Romanchuk & McFadden) — Sports/Para-athletics; (3) EXCAP home-exercise program reduces "chemo brain"; (4) 40-million-year-old ant revealed in Goethe's amber via synchrotron CT.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Sudan & South Sudan eliminate maternal & neonatal tetanus; (2) Wheelchair half-marathon records in Chicago (Romanchuk & McFadden) — Sports/Para-athletics; (3) EXCAP home-exercise program reduces "chemo brain"; (4) 40-million-year-old ant revealed in Goethe's amber via synchrotron CT.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, June 10, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Cobalt's hidden topological states; (2) Floreana giant tortoise returns; (3) Four first-time World Cup nations; (4) China's electric heavy trucks tip.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Cobalt's hidden topological states; (2) Floreana giant tortoise returns; (3) Four first-time World Cup nations; (4) China's electric heavy trucks tip.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, June 9, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) AI-designed universal coronavirus vaccine clears first human trial; (2) Low-temperature hydrogen catalyst cuts cost; (3) "House Made of Dawn" — largest Native American art survey at the New-York Historical — Culture/Museums; (4) Three Stanley Cup Final records in one game.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) AI-designed universal coronavirus vaccine clears first human trial; (2) Low-temperature hydrogen catalyst cuts cost; (3) "House Made of Dawn" — largest Native American art survey at the New-York Historical — Culture/Museums; (4) Three Stanley Cup Final records in one game.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, June 8, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Ocean protection crosses 10%; (2) Europe's largest 3D-printed affordable apartment building; (3) LEGO Foundation pledges $97M to the IRC for kids in conflict zones; (4) Two molecules repaired nerve damage in MS models.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Ocean protection crosses 10%; (2) Europe's largest 3D-printed affordable apartment building; (3) LEGO Foundation pledges $97M to the IRC for kids in conflict zones; (4) Two molecules repaired nerve damage in MS models.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, June 7, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Octopuses use mirrors to find food they can't see; (2) Europe sets a new dam-removal record; (3) Roman bridge found in the Aare riverbed; (4) Mirra Andreeva wins the French Open.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Octopuses use mirrors to find food they can't see; (2) Europe sets a new dam-removal record; (3) Roman bridge found in the Aare riverbed; (4) Mirra Andreeva wins the French Open.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, June 6, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) "Another Wonderland": rescued WPA Alice murals open at MCNY; (2) MLK Center opens North End satellite food pantry, Newport RI; (3) PRIMA retinal implant restores central vision in advanced AMD; (4) EPFL shrinks a tabletop ultrafast laser onto a chip.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) "Another Wonderland": rescued WPA Alice murals open at MCNY; (2) MLK Center opens North End satellite food pantry, Newport RI; (3) PRIMA retinal implant restores central vision in advanced AMD; (4) EPFL shrinks a tabletop ultrafast laser onto a chip.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, June 5, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) First evidence of magnetic fields on exoplanets; (2) 5,500-year-old high-altitude copper-working cave, Pyrenees; (3) Goya's San Antonio de la Florida frescoes restored, Madrid; (4) Amivantamab shrinks advanced head & neck cancer (OrigAMI-4).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) First evidence of magnetic fields on exoplanets; (2) 5,500-year-old high-altitude copper-working cave, Pyrenees; (3) Goya's San Antonio de la Florida frescoes restored, Madrid; (4) Amivantamab shrinks advanced head & neck cancer (OrigAMI-4).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, June 4, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) ASKAP J1745 — the Rosetta Stone for long-period radio transients; (2) Red-cockaded woodpecker — record 2026 nesting count in Virginia; (3) NFIL3 — a CRISPR fix for CAR T-cell exhaustion against solid tumors; (4) McKendree — first-ever NCAA Division II softball national champion.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) ASKAP J1745 — the Rosetta Stone for long-period radio transients; (2) Red-cockaded woodpecker — record 2026 nesting count in Virginia; (3) NFIL3 — a CRISPR fix for CAR T-cell exhaustion against solid tumors; (4) McKendree — first-ever NCAA Division II softball national champion.

item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Headline:; (2) Headline:; (3) Headline:; (4) Headline:.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, June 2, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Daraxonrasib doubles survival in metastatic pancreatic cancer; (2) Zero-waste solar desalination — University of Rochester; (3) Seini synagogue restoration completed — becomes Museum of the Jews of Transylvania; (4) Solar overtakes coal on the Texas (ERCOT) grid — first time annually.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Daraxonrasib doubles survival in metastatic pancreatic cancer; (2) Zero-waste solar desalination — University of Rochester; (3) Seini synagogue restoration completed — becomes Museum of the Jews of Transylvania; (4) Solar overtakes coal on the Texas (ERCOT) grid — first time annually.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, June 1, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Joao Fonseca beats Novak Djokovic, Roland-Garros R3; (2) Ancient DNA: women spread farming into NW Europe via marriage; (3) Kank australis — new heron-niche raptor dinosaur from Patagonia; (4) Iowa teacher Kim Rohlf makes custom quilts for every student before retirement.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Joao Fonseca beats Novak Djokovic, Roland-Garros R3; (2) Ancient DNA: women spread farming into NW Europe via marriage; (3) Kank australis — new heron-niche raptor dinosaur from Patagonia; (4) Iowa teacher Kim Rohlf makes custom quilts for every student before retirement.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, May 31, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Saturn's rotation puzzle solved by JWST (Science/Planetary); (2) Anacostia Community Museum opens "We Make History" with the Plummer family diary at its center (Community/Heritage); (3) Platypuses breeding again in Australia's Hacking River after 50+ year absence (Environment/Conservation); (4) England's first Dutch-style "fietsstraat" cycle street opens in Cambridge (Innovation/Urban).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Saturn's rotation puzzle solved by JWST (Science/Planetary); (2) Anacostia Community Museum opens "We Make History" with the Plummer family diary at its center (Community/Heritage); (3) Platypuses breeding again in Australia's Hacking River after 50+ year absence (Environment/Conservation); (4) England's first Dutch-style "fietsstraat" cycle street opens in Cambridge (Innovation/Urban).

item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Category:; (2) Category:; (3) Category:; (4) Category:.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, May 29, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Superconductivity record at 151K, ambient pressure; (2) Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks Chris Gayle's 14-year IPL sixes record; (3) Mike Gregg's $10M / 17,000-acre Australian wildlife sanctuary; (4) Mister Rogers stamp reissued via USPS public vote.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Superconductivity record at 151K, ambient pressure; (2) Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks Chris Gayle's 14-year IPL sixes record; (3) Mike Gregg's $10M / 17,000-acre Australian wildlife sanctuary; (4) Mister Rogers stamp reissued via USPS public vote.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, May 28, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) LEAD — First gene therapy for genetic deafness (Otarmeni) is approved, and free in the US; (2) Knicks reach first NBA Finals since 1999; (3) AI recycling robots recover materials we used to bury; (4) Main Street America posts record downtown reinvestment.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) LEAD — First gene therapy for genetic deafness (Otarmeni) is approved, and free in the US; (2) Knicks reach first NBA Finals since 1999; (3) AI recycling robots recover materials we used to bury; (4) Main Street America posts record downtown reinvestment.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, May 27, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Felix Rosenqvist wins the closest Indy 500 in history; (2) Grid battery storage hits a record 112 GW in 2025; (3) UT Southwestern finds HELZ2, a liver "dimmer" on bad cholesterol; (4) Big Bear kids raising millions to buy and protect open land.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Felix Rosenqvist wins the closest Indy 500 in history; (2) Grid battery storage hits a record 112 GW in 2025; (3) UT Southwestern finds HELZ2, a liver "dimmer" on bad cholesterol; (4) Big Bear kids raising millions to buy and protect open land.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, May 26, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A seven-cent teabag that removes arsenic from drinking water; (2) Baltimore records its fewest monthly homicides since 1970; (3) A failed communist megaproject becomes Bucharest's largest green space; (4) The first large-scale genetic map of cancer in cats.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) A seven-cent teabag that removes arsenic from drinking water; (2) Baltimore records its fewest monthly homicides since 1970; (3) A failed communist megaproject becomes Bucharest's largest green space; (4) The first large-scale genetic map of cancer in cats.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, May 25, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) LEAD — Health/Public Health: Tunisia eliminates trachoma; (2) Innovation/Accessibility: Assistive AI crosses into daily life; (3) Community/Education: Chicago Class of 2026 leaves with college underway; (4) Culture/Heritage: Oldest English poem found in a Roman library.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) LEAD — Health/Public Health: Tunisia eliminates trachoma; (2) Innovation/Accessibility: Assistive AI crosses into daily life; (3) Community/Education: Chicago Class of 2026 leaves with college underway; (4) Culture/Heritage: Oldest English poem found in a Roman library.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, May 24, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) GLP-1 drugs cut cardiovascular risk — and not just by trimming weight; (2) A platinum-free catalyst moves clean hydrogen toward "buildable"; (3) Arsenal win the Premier League — first title in 22 years; (4) The Quentin Blake Centre for Illustration opens — a first permanent home for the art form.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) GLP-1 drugs cut cardiovascular risk — and not just by trimming weight; (2) A platinum-free catalyst moves clean hydrogen toward "buildable"; (3) Arsenal win the Premier League — first title in 22 years; (4) The Quentin Blake Centre for Illustration opens — a first permanent home for the art form.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, May 23, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) AI tool confirms 118 new exoplanets hidden in NASA's TESS data; (2) Dutch court rules bottom trawling in a marine protected area unlawful; (3) Knicks erase a 22-point deficit to win Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals; (4) Good Neighbor Day America: a coordinated national day of service.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) AI tool confirms 118 new exoplanets hidden in NASA's TESS data; (2) Dutch court rules bottom trawling in a marine protected area unlawful; (3) Knicks erase a 22-point deficit to win Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals; (4) Good Neighbor Day America: a coordinated national day of service.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, May 17, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Malaria vaccine averts 1 in 8 child deaths in first rollout countries; (2) California coho salmon return — 30,000 to Mendocino coast, naturally reproducing in upper Russian River; (3) Deep canyons off Western Australia reveal hidden marine life; (4) Cumberland, BC repurposing abandoned coal-mine groundwater for geothermal.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Malaria vaccine averts 1 in 8 child deaths in first rollout countries; (2) California coho salmon return — 30,000 to Mendocino coast, naturally reproducing in upper Russian River; (3) Deep canyons off Western Australia reveal hidden marine life; (4) Cumberland, BC repurposing abandoned coal-mine groundwater for geothermal.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, May 16, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) NHS rolls out a one-minute immunotherapy injection (Health/Medicine); (2) 108 school districts quietly bucking the national reading slide (Community/Education); (3) Cleveland Orchestra opens the Mandel Festival on the theme of courage (Culture/Arts); (4) The Chinese money plant turns out to be doing Voronoi geometry (Science/Nature).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) NHS rolls out a one-minute immunotherapy injection (Health/Medicine); (2) 108 school districts quietly bucking the national reading slide (Community/Education); (3) Cleveland Orchestra opens the Mandel Festival on the theme of courage (Culture/Arts); (4) The Chinese money plant turns out to be doing Voronoi geometry (Science/Nature).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, May 15, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) New Zealand's kākāpō has its best breeding season ever (Environment / Conservation); (2) Wings for Life World Run 2026 sets new global record (Sports / Community); (3) JUPITER supercomputer sets new world record with 50-qubit quantum simulation (Innovation / Science); (4) A 6-year-old in Norway found a 1,300-year-old Viking-era sword on a school trip (Culture / Archaeology / Discovery).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) New Zealand's kākāpō has its best breeding season ever (Environment / Conservation); (2) Wings for Life World Run 2026 sets new global record (Sports / Community); (3) JUPITER supercomputer sets new world record with 50-qubit quantum simulation (Innovation / Science); (4) A 6-year-old in Norway found a 1,300-year-old Viking-era sword on a school trip (Culture / Archaeology / Discovery).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, May 14, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Hidden molecular switch in brown fat could change weight and bone health together; (2) KehatiKu pays Borneo villagers to record wildlife — and the math of the forest changes; (3) Australian researchers turn plastic waste into clean hydrogen using sunlight; (4) NASA fires up most powerful electric thruster ever tested in the US — a step toward crewed Mars.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Hidden molecular switch in brown fat could change weight and bone health together; (2) KehatiKu pays Borneo villagers to record wildlife — and the math of the forest changes; (3) Australian researchers turn plastic waste into clean hydrogen using sunlight; (4) NASA fires up most powerful electric thruster ever tested in the US — a step toward crewed Mars.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, May 13, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Cape Verde becomes the smallest country by land area ever to qualify for the FIFA World Cup (Sports); (2) U.S. renewables generated more electricity than natural gas for an entire month, for the first time on record (Innovation/Energy); (3) Direct Prague–Copenhagen passenger rail returns after more than a decade (Innovation/Infrastructure); (4) A Wisconsin junior keeps a 14-year promise and takes his grandmother to prom (Community/Human Interest).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Cape Verde becomes the smallest country by land area ever to qualify for the FIFA World Cup (Sports); (2) U.S. renewables generated more electricity than natural gas for an entire month, for the first time on record (Innovation/Energy); (3) Direct Prague–Copenhagen passenger rail returns after more than a decade (Innovation/Infrastructure); (4) A Wisconsin junior keeps a 14-year promise and takes his grandmother to prom (Community/Human Interest).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, May 12, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) North Atlantic right whale calving season produces 23 calves — highest in 15 years (Environment / Conservation); (2) Hanoi removes fences from Thong Nhat Park — its biggest park is now fully open public space (Environment / Community); (3) Women's Pro Baseball League announces inaugural-season schedule — first U.S. women's pro baseball since 1954 (Sports / Culture); (4) Colorado bans pet stores from selling dogs and cats — redirects the puppy-mill pipeline (Community / Animal Welfare).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) North Atlantic right whale calving season produces 23 calves — highest in 15 years (Environment / Conservation); (2) Hanoi removes fences from Thong Nhat Park — its biggest park is now fully open public space (Environment / Community); (3) Women's Pro Baseball League announces inaugural-season schedule — first U.S. women's pro baseball since 1954 (Sports / Culture); (4) Colorado bans pet stores from selling dogs and cats — redirects the puppy-mill pipeline (Community / Animal Welfare).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, May 11, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Sir David Attenborough turns 100; (2) Roeper School wins third consecutive forensics state title; (3) Common constipation drug shows promise slowing chronic kidney disease; (4) Two new American central libraries open this week.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Sir David Attenborough turns 100; (2) Roeper School wins third consecutive forensics state title; (3) Common constipation drug shows promise slowing chronic kidney disease; (4) Two new American central libraries open this week.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, May 10, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Lighthouse of Alexandria — 22 massive stone blocks lifted from the Mediterranean seafloor (Culture/Archaeology); (2) USA Pickleball + Boys & Girls Clubs of America — national partnership for youth access (Sports/Community); (3) "Vulnerable to Vibrant" Houston — 50 residents graduate as solar installers (Community/Innovation/Energy); (4) Sameer Santana, age 6, performs Heimlich on choking classmate — Stephen Olney Elementary, North Providence, Rhode Island (Community/Human Interest).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Lighthouse of Alexandria — 22 massive stone blocks lifted from the Mediterranean seafloor (Culture/Archaeology); (2) USA Pickleball + Boys & Girls Clubs of America — national partnership for youth access (Sports/Community); (3) "Vulnerable to Vibrant" Houston — 50 residents graduate as solar installers (Community/Innovation/Energy); (4) Sameer Santana, age 6, performs Heimlich on choking classmate — Stephen Olney Elementary, North Providence, Rhode Island (Community/Human Interest).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, May 9, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Bar Harbor third-graders raise money for a friendship bench; (2) Challenged Athletes Foundation 2026 grants — $7M to 4,033 athletes; (3) Libraro prize — six unpublished novels chosen by readers, not gatekeepers; (4) Sweden — more reliable insulin-producing cells from stem cells.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Bar Harbor third-graders raise money for a friendship bench; (2) Challenged Athletes Foundation 2026 grants — $7M to 4,033 athletes; (3) Libraro prize — six unpublished novels chosen by readers, not gatekeepers; (4) Sweden — more reliable insulin-producing cells from stem cells.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, May 8, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Mexico's jaguar population grew about 30% in fourteen years (Environment / Conservation); (2) Pre-surgery pembrolizumab keeps a colorectal-cancer subset cancer-free (Science / Health); (3) Center for Black Excellence and Culture opens in Madison, WI (Culture / Community); (4) Jude Baker, 14, redirected his wish-grant to feed hundreds of people experiencing homelessness (Community / Human Interest).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Mexico's jaguar population grew about 30% in fourteen years (Environment / Conservation); (2) Pre-surgery pembrolizumab keeps a colorectal-cancer subset cancer-free (Science / Health); (3) Center for Black Excellence and Culture opens in Madison, WI (Culture / Community); (4) Jude Baker, 14, redirected his wish-grant to feed hundreds of people experiencing homelessness (Community / Human Interest).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, May 7, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Madhusmita Jena sets Guinness World Record for fastest marathon in a saree; (2) Girls' high school flag football grows 60% in one year, 17 states sanction; (3) Social prescribing rises during Mental Health Awareness Month; (4) Boosting Sox9 protein wakes brain's astrocytes to clear Alzheimer's plaques.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Madhusmita Jena sets Guinness World Record for fastest marathon in a saree; (2) Girls' high school flag football grows 60% in one year, 17 states sanction; (3) Social prescribing rises during Mental Health Awareness Month; (4) Boosting Sox9 protein wakes brain's astrocytes to clear Alzheimer's plaques.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, May 6, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Cherie DeVaux becomes first female trainer to win the Kentucky Derby; (2) JAMA study links 988 crisis-line launch to ~11% drop in young-adult suicide deaths; (3) Malawian innovator wins ICT Award for AI eye-control system for motor disabilities; (4) Yangtze River fish biomass doubles after sweeping 2021 fishing ban.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Cherie DeVaux becomes first female trainer to win the Kentucky Derby; (2) JAMA study links 988 crisis-line launch to ~11% drop in young-adult suicide deaths; (3) Malawian innovator wins ICT Award for AI eye-control system for motor disabilities; (4) Yangtze River fish biomass doubles after sweeping 2021 fishing ban.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, May 5, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Crabs' famous sideways walk traces to a single 200-million-year-old evolutionary moment (Science); (2) Soil microbe fuel cell powers underground sensors without batteries or solar (Innovation/Energy); (3) Two University of Melbourne engineering students recreate Sir William Hamilton's 1775 mechanical volcano (Culture/Science); (4) An Ohio woman bought the wrong lottery ticket by mistake — and won $1,852,212 (Community/Quirky).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Crabs' famous sideways walk traces to a single 200-million-year-old evolutionary moment (Science); (2) Soil microbe fuel cell powers underground sensors without batteries or solar (Innovation/Energy); (3) Two University of Melbourne engineering students recreate Sir William Hamilton's 1775 mechanical volcano (Culture/Science); (4) An Ohio woman bought the wrong lottery ticket by mistake — and won $1,852,212 (Community/Quirky).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, May 4, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Detroit Pistons erase a 24-point deficit to force Game 7 against Orlando (Sports); (2) Over 100 ancient game boards carved into the ruins of Ptolemais (Culture/Arts/Archaeology); (3) FDA approves Otarmeni — first gene therapy for a form of inherited deafness, provided free of charge in the U.S. (Innovation/Health); (4) Ancient Maya communities traded live dogs across hundreds of miles, fed them human-quality food (Culture/Science).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Detroit Pistons erase a 24-point deficit to force Game 7 against Orlando (Sports); (2) Over 100 ancient game boards carved into the ruins of Ptolemais (Culture/Arts/Archaeology); (3) FDA approves Otarmeni — first gene therapy for a form of inherited deafness, provided free of charge in the U.S. (Innovation/Health); (4) Ancient Maya communities traded live dogs across hundreds of miles, fed them human-quality food (Culture/Science).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Sunday, May 3, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Sir Henry Raeburn's lost portrait of Robert Burns rediscovered after 200+ years (Culture/Arts); (2) Vaccine cyclists deliver HPV vaccines to remote villages in rural Zimbabwe (Community/Health); (3) McLaren Racing + Great Barrier Reef Foundation unveil "Machine One" — coral seeding goes industrial (Environment/Innovation); (4) CATL launches the world's first mass-production sodium-ion EV (Innovation/Energy).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Sir Henry Raeburn's lost portrait of Robert Burns rediscovered after 200+ years (Culture/Arts); (2) Vaccine cyclists deliver HPV vaccines to remote villages in rural Zimbabwe (Community/Health); (3) McLaren Racing + Great Barrier Reef Foundation unveil "Machine One" — coral seeding goes industrial (Environment/Innovation); (4) CATL launches the world's first mass-production sodium-ion EV (Innovation/Energy).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Saturday, May 2, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Six-year-old Saffie Sandford has her sight restored by NHS gene therapy (Health/Medicine); (2) World-first footage: a Sumatran orangutan crosses a canopy bridge in Indonesia (Environment/Conservation); (3) A 23-year-old Polish streamer raises over $68 million for kids with cancer in nine days (Community/Generosity); (4) DESI completes the largest, sharpest 3D map of the universe ever made (Science/Space).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Six-year-old Saffie Sandford has her sight restored by NHS gene therapy (Health/Medicine); (2) World-first footage: a Sumatran orangutan crosses a canopy bridge in Indonesia (Environment/Conservation); (3) A 23-year-old Polish streamer raises over $68 million for kids with cancer in nine days (Community/Generosity); (4) DESI completes the largest, sharpest 3D map of the universe ever made (Science/Space).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Friday, May 1, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) The Paiter Surui Lead Conservation Lessons in the Amazon — WWF feature; (2) The "Call a Boomer" Payphone — Boston-to-Reno Line for Generational Loneliness; (3) Hamburg Undergrads Build a Cavity Detector and Set New Limits on Dark Matter; (4) A 100-Year-Old Problem in Organ Preservation Just Got Smaller.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) The Paiter Surui Lead Conservation Lessons in the Amazon — WWF feature; (2) The "Call a Boomer" Payphone — Boston-to-Reno Line for Generational Loneliness; (3) Hamburg Undergrads Build a Cavity Detector and Set New Limits on Dark Matter; (4) A 100-Year-Old Problem in Organ Preservation Just Got Smaller.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Thursday, April 30, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Book Fairies + ThriftBooks deliver 25,000 books to NYC kids (Community); (2) Camera-trap photos turn up an unexpected refuge for endangered Asian tapirs in Thailand (Environment); (3) 132-million-year-old dinosaur tracks discovered on South Africa's coast — youngest ever in southern Africa (Culture / Discovery); (4) A 30-year, 100,000-person study finds variety in exercise — not just amount — extends life (Science/Health).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Book Fairies + ThriftBooks deliver 25,000 books to NYC kids (Community); (2) Camera-trap photos turn up an unexpected refuge for endangered Asian tapirs in Thailand (Environment); (3) 132-million-year-old dinosaur tracks discovered on South Africa's coast — youngest ever in southern Africa (Culture / Discovery); (4) A 30-year, 100,000-person study finds variety in exercise — not just amount — extends life (Science/Health).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Wednesday, April 29, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) London Marathon: First sub-2-hour official marathon (Sports); (2) Lusatian Lakeland: Final coal-mine-turned-lake opens (Environment); (3) Iliad found inside Roman-era mummy at Oxyrhynchus (Culture/Archaeology); (4) Rock climbing as therapy for Parkinson's disease (Health/Innovation).
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) London Marathon: First sub-2-hour official marathon (Sports); (2) Lusatian Lakeland: Final coal-mine-turned-lake opens (Environment); (3) Iliad found inside Roman-era mummy at Oxyrhynchus (Culture/Archaeology); (4) Rock climbing as therapy for Parkinson's disease (Health/Innovation).

item.title: The Morning Lift — Tuesday, April 28, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) 6,500 volunteers build 10,000 beds in 24 hours for kids who don't have one — Charlotte, NC; (2) NASA's Curiosity rover finds the strongest chemical signature yet of an ancient Martian lake; (3) A 1,300-year-old maize god sculpture, found by surprise during a backyard dig in Tlaxcala; (4) The largest clean-energy project in U.S. history starts sending power — SunZia.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) 6,500 volunteers build 10,000 beds in 24 hours for kids who don't have one — Charlotte, NC; (2) NASA's Curiosity rover finds the strongest chemical signature yet of an ancient Martian lake; (3) A 1,300-year-old maize god sculpture, found by surprise during a backyard dig in Tlaxcala; (4) The largest clean-energy project in U.S. history starts sending power — SunZia.

item.title: The Morning Lift — Monday, April 27, 2026
item.description: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Kākāpō record-breaking breeding season — 100+ chicks hatched in New Zealand; (2) Kruunuvuorensilta — Finland's longest bridge, car-free, opens to pedestrians and cyclists; (3) UK new electric cars cheaper than petrol for the first time — Autotrader data; (4) Sonselasuchus cedrus — ancient crocodile relative that switched to two legs as it grew.
item.itunes:summary: Four positive stories to start your morning: (1) Kākāpō record-breaking breeding season — 100+ chicks hatched in New Zealand; (2) Kruunuvuorensilta — Finland's longest bridge, car-free, opens to pedestrians and cyclists; (3) UK new electric cars cheaper than petrol for the first time — Autotrader data; (4) Sonselasuchus cedrus — ancient crocodile relative that switched to two legs as it grew.
